chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Petition No.D-356 of 2023 Date Order with signature of Judge 1. For orders on office objection at flag `A` 2. For hearing of CMA No.1612/2023 3. For hearing of main case 19-09-2023 M/s Muhammad Iqbal Memon and Syed Muhammad Ali Shah Advocates for the Petitioner Mr. Ali Raza Baloch, Assistant Advocate General.-.-.-. -.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro,J:- Petitioner is a public servant working in BS-20 and serving as a Professor in Education department. She is aggrieved of her transfer vide a Notification dated 21.02.2023 whereby she has been posted in Government Girls Degree College J.M Brohi Goth, Gushan-e-Maymar Karachi against an existing vacancy. Her grounds are that she is originally resident of Sukkur; she is at the verge of retirement (November 2023); she has got a fracture in her leg and no one from her family is living in Karachi with whom she can live and perform her duty peacefully. Notwithstanding merit of her grounds and the inconvenience, tribulation, petitioner is undergoing on account of the impugned Notification transferring her from Pano Akil to Karachi, we cannot exercise our jurisdiction in her favour in the light of bar under Article 212 of the Constitution of Pakistan, as the transfer comes within the terms and conditions of the service. Her efforts before the Service Tribunal questioning her transfer has already met a failure. Therefore, we with the consent of the learned AAG dispose of this petition and direct the Chief Secretary, where we are informed, the petitioner has already filed an appeal against her transfer to consider her case on humanitarian grounds and decide the application within fifteen days, for the relief she is seeking through this petition, in accordance with law.

Her efforts before the Service Tribunal questioning her transfer has already met a failure. Therefore, we with the consent of the learned AAG dispose of this petition and direct the Chief Secretary, where we are informed, the petitioner has already filed an appeal against her transfer to consider her case on humanitarian grounds and decide the application within fifteen days, for the relief she is seeking through this petition, in accordance with law. Accordingly this petition is disposed of. JUDGE JUDGE Sulemen Khan/PA 2
